message:
Last week my 8-year-old asked why we have a 'boring emergency kit' in the garage. I said, 'So we're ready if something unexpected happens.' She looked at me dead serious and said, 'Like when you can't find your phone?' 😄

Kids have a way of keeping us honest. She's right that most days feel pretty normal—and they usually are. But I realized I'd never actually *shown* her what's in that kit or why it matters. So we opened it up together, talked about what our family would do if we needed to leave quickly or stay put, and suddenly it felt way less 'boring' and more like a plan we're all part of.

Turns out when kids understand the why, they get invested. And honestly? It helped me remember this isn't about doom-scrolling or worst-case scenarios. It's just about loving your people enough to think ahead.

Have you talked through a simple emergency plan with your family yet? What made it click for them?
